borrowed: Use browser tests for focused accessibility assertions and pair automation with manual review notes.
Scope scans and assertions to visible UI state after controls reveal drawers or panels.
rejected: Do not treat automated accessibility checks as full WCAG certification.

borrowed: Record stable accessible structure around the cockpit root, table, inspector, and drawer.
rejected: Do not snapshot volatile table text as the only accessibility proof.

borrowed: Capture desktop and responsive screenshots as visual baselines for the cockpit.
rejected: Do not use pixel comparison alone for layout truth; pair it with overflow assertions.

sources.source: Playwright browser contexts and trace viewer
borrowed: Use isolated browser contexts for URL restoration and filter state tests.
rejected: Do not share mutable browser state across route and preview tests.

sources.source: NHS digital service manual content guide
borrowed: Keep operational copy clear, consistent, and practical for service users.
rejected: Do not add marketing or decorative hero language to an internal release cockpit.

sources.source: NHS digital service manual layout guidance
borrowed: Respect readable line lengths and transactional layout restraint.
rejected: Do not force patient-facing NHS page width on this internal dense operations board.

borrowed: Use native table semantics for tabular route data and explicit tabs for inspector sections.
rejected: Do not implement a custom grid when native table navigation is enough for this read-heavy surface.

borrowed: Reduce visual noise while preserving hierarchy and density in sidebar, headers, and panels.
Stress dense list, split, and side-panel states before implementation.
rejected: Do not import Linear's dark, product-branded chrome into an NHS release-control surface.

borrowed: Use persistent nested layout thinking so route, inspector, and preview state survive navigation.
rejected: Do not introduce Next.js routing into this Vite ops-console app.

borrowed: Dense tables need clear toolbar/filter affordances and stable row scanning.
rejected: Do not copy Carbon styling; only borrow structured data inspection principles.
